revision.text:
[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: - Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto - Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto - Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: - Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto - Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto - Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami

In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: - Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto - Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto - Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.

[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: - Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto - Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto - Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>

[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=draft}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=draft}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=draft}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所|12=draft}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=draft}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|12=draft}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄|12=draft}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|12=draft}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|12=draft}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=draft}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=draft}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=draft}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=draft}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所|12=draft}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=draft}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|12=draft}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市|12=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄|12=draft}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|12=draft}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|12=draft}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=安曇野市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[安曇野市]]三郷温5932番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[住吉三神]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]、広籏八幡大神 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[お船祭り]]（毎年4月） |神事 = }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[安曇野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。
== 祭神 == 祭神は以下の5柱<ref>長野県町村誌 1876年</ref>。 *[[住吉三神]] **底筒男命 **中筒男命 **表筒男命 *[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] *広籏八幡大神
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の東征の際に創建され、境内社に坂上田村明神が祀られている。[[平安時代]]後期に、当地の開発領主西牧氏の寄進によって[[長講堂領]][[住吉荘]]が成立すると、荘内十八郷の総鎮守となった。当初は[[烏川 (長野県)|烏川]]渓谷の角蔵山の尾根の麓にあったとされ、現在も宵祭のために神官や氏子総代が登頂する。古城山には御旅所があり、例祭の翌日には神馬を立てて神事を行った。
[[長徳]]年間には[[信濃国|信濃国司]]藤原惟正が社殿を修築し、[[武田氏]]時代の[[永禄]]10年（1567年）には氏子が棟銭10銭を出し合って社殿を造営した記録があり、江戸時代以降は楡村や及木村など周辺諸村の住民によって造営され、現在の社殿は[[天明]]6年（1786年）のもので安曇野市の指定文化財となっている。
[[文禄]]時代の検地では社領7石が安堵されているが、これは[[安曇郡]]内では[[穂高神社]]に次ぐ処遇であった。また[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（1615年）には新田開発に田三反歩が寄進されている。[[享保]]9年（1724年）の記録によれば、東西2町48間、南北4町24間（44,352坪）の広大な境内を持っていたことが知られる。
毎年4月には[[お船祭り]]が行われ、壮大な山車（お船）が曳航される<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り]安曇野市</ref>。
== 文化財 == *本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *古文書（安曇野市有形文化財） *お船祭り（安曇野市無形民俗文化財） *社叢（安曇野市天然記念物） *御神木「ヒノキ」（安曇野市天然記念物）
== 所在地 == * 長野県安曇野市三郷温5932番地
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』 2000年 [[白水社]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや あつみの}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:安曇野市の歴史]] [[Category:安曇野市の建築物]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:坂上田村麻呂|社すみよししんしや けいたいしや さかのうえのたむらみようしん]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{神社 |名称 = 住吉神社 |画像 ={{画像募集中|cat=安曇野市}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[安曇野市]]三郷温5932番地 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000}} |祭神 = [[住吉三神]]、[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]、広籏八幡大神 |社格 = 県社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[お船祭り]]（毎年4月） |神事 = }} '''住吉神社'''（すみよしじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[安曇野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。
== 祭神 == 祭神は以下の5柱<ref>長野県町村誌 1876年</ref>。 *[[住吉三神]] **底筒男命 **中筒男命 **表筒男命 *[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]] *広籏八幡大神
== 歴史 == 社伝によれば、[[坂上田村麻呂]]の東征の際に創建され、境内社に坂上田村明神が祀られている。[[平安時代]]後期に、当地の開発領主西牧氏の寄進によって[[長講堂領]][[住吉荘]]が成立すると、荘内十八郷の総鎮守となった。当初は[[烏川 (長野県)|烏川]]渓谷の角蔵山の尾根の麓にあったとされ、現在も宵祭のために神官や氏子総代が登頂する。古城山には御旅所があり、例祭の翌日には神馬を立てて神事を行った。
[[長徳]]年間には[[信濃国|信濃国司]]藤原惟正が社殿を修築し、[[武田氏]]時代の[[永禄]]10年（1567年）には氏子が棟銭10銭を出し合って社殿を造営した記録があり、江戸時代以降は楡村や及木村など周辺諸村の住民によって造営され、現在の社殿は[[天明]]6年（1786年）のもので安曇野市の指定文化財となっている。
[[文禄]]時代の検地では社領7石が安堵されているが、これは[[安曇郡]]内では[[穂高神社]]に次ぐ処遇であった。また[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（1615年）には新田開発に田三反歩が寄進されている。[[享保]]9年（1724年）の記録によれば、東西2町48間、南北4町24間（44,352坪）の広大な境内を持っていたことが知られる。
毎年4月には[[お船祭り]]が行われ、壮大な山車（お船）が曳航される<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html 熊野神社のお船祭り]安曇野市</ref>。
== 文化財 == *本殿（安曇野市有形文化財） *古文書（安曇野市有形文化財） *お船祭り（安曇野市無形民俗文化財） *社叢（安曇野市天然記念物） *御神木「ヒノキ」（安曇野市天然記念物）
== 所在地 == * 長野県安曇野市三郷温5932番地
== 脚注 == <references/> == 参考文献 == *『信州の文化シリーズ 寺と神社』 1981年 [[信濃毎日新聞|信濃毎日新聞社]] *[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』 2000年 [[白水社]]
{{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=長野県}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すみよししんしや あつみの}} [[Category:長野県の旧県社]] [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:安曇野市の歴史]] [[Category:安曇野市の建築物]] [[Category:住吉神社]] [[Category:坂上田村麻呂|社すみよししんしや けいたいしや さかのうえのたむらみようしん]] [[Category:長野県内の市町村指定有形文化財]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.

Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|ja|坂上田村麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|qq=}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|otabisho|ja|御旅所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/otabisho|qq=}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku era|ja|長徳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku era|qq=}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|ja|武田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda clan|qq=}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino City|ja|安曇野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumino City|qq=}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku era|ja|文禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku era|qq=}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|ja|穂高神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hotaka Shrine|qq=}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → History of Azumino
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]
