{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|16|6.39|N|137|52|46.95|E|scale:30000|display=inline}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>

Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|qq=|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|qq=|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876386]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.268442|N|137.879708|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = | location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876386]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.268442|N|137.879708|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = | location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876386]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.268442|N|137.879708|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = | location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q85876386}} [[Category:Buildings in Azumino]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Azumino]] [[Category:Image requests in Azumino City]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876386]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.268442|N|137.879708|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = | location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano]], [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of [[:en:Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro]], and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An [[:en:Otabisho|otabisho]] (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the [[:en:Chōtoku|Chōtoku era]], the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda clan]], parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of [[:en:Azumino|Azumino City]].
In the [[:en:Bunroku|Bunroku era]] land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the [[:en:Hotaka Shrine|Hotaka Shrine]] within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

In the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku era}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}
== Related Articles == * [[Sumiyoshi Shrines in Japan]] * [[Ofune Matsuri]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:住吉神社 (安曇野市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q85876386]] Wikidata name: Sumiyoshi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sumiyoshi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image requested|cat=Azumino City}} | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}, [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Misato-On 5932 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.268442|N|137.879708|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deities = [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]], [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]], Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami | type = Former Prefectural Shrine | Established = | Main Hall = | Alias = | festival = [[Ofune Matsuri]] (April each year) | Rites = | location = {{ill|Azumino|WD=Q534667}} }} '''Sumiyoshi Shrine''' (住吉神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano}}, [[:en:Azumino City|Azumino]], Japan. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|modern shrine Ranking system]], it was classified as a former Prefectural Shrine.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates five deities<ref>Nagano Prefecture Township Records, 1876</ref>: * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]]: ** Sokotsutsuo no Mikoto ** Nakatsutsuo no Mikoto ** Uwatsutsuo no Mikoto * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Mikoto]] * Hirohata Hachiman Ōkami
== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=otabisho}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku|en|Chōtoku|WD=Q1327213|lt=Chōtoku era}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda clan}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino City}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku era}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

== History == According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established during the eastern campaign of {{ill|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|en|Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|WD=Q941979|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}}, and the subordinate shrine on the grounds enshrines {{ill|Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja|坂上田村明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue Tamura Myōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the late {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the shrine became the total guardian of the 18 villages within the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-shō|ja|住吉荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi-shō|qq=}}, which was established through the donations of the local ruling Nishimaki clan when the {{ill|Chōkōdō|ja|長講堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōkōdō|1=Chōkō-dō Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653663}} manor was created. Originally, the shrine is said to have been located at the base of the ridge of Kakuzōzan Mountain in the {{ill|Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|ja|烏川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu River (Nagano Prefecture)|1=Q11567223|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567223}} gorge, where shrine priests and representatives of parishioners still climb during the Yoi Matsuri (Evening Festival). An {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|WD=Q11489117|lt=otabisho|1=Otabisho}} (temporary shrine) remains at Furushiroyama, where sacred horse rituals were performed the day after the annual festival.
During the {{ill|Chōtoku|en|Chōtoku|WD=Q1327213|lt=Chōtoku era|1=Chōtoku}}, the {{ill|Shinano Provincial Governor|ja|信濃国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Provincial Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Fujiwara Koremasa renovated the shrine buildings. Records show that in 1567 (Eiroku 10), during the era of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda clan|1=Takeda clan}}, parishioners contributed ten coins each to reconstruct the shrine buildings. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was maintained by residents of nearby villages such as Nire and Obiki, and the current shrine buildings date to 1786 (Tenmei 6). These are now designated as cultural properties of {{ill|Azumino|en|Azumino|WD=Q534667|lt=Azumino City|1=Azumino}}.
In the {{ill|Bunroku|en|Bunroku|WD=Q1373039|lt=Bunroku era|1=Bunroku}} land survey, the shrine's land holdings of seven koku were guaranteed, making it second only to the {{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|WD=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} within the Azumi District in terms of status. Additionally, in 1615 (Genwa 1), three tanbu of rice paddies were donated for new land development. Records from 1724 (Kyōhō 9) indicate that the shrine once had an expansive precinct measuring 2 chō and 48 ken east to west and 4 chō and 24 ken north to south (approximately 146,176 square meters).
Every April, the {{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|お船祭り|1=Ofune Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}} is held, featuring the grand towing of a majestic festival float, or "ship."<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Ofune Matsuri of Kumano Shrine] - Azumino City</ref>
== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.
== Cultural Properties == * '''Main Hall''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Historical Documents''' (Designated Tangible Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Ofune Matsuri''' (Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Azumino City) * '''Shrine Forest''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City) * '''Sacred Tree: Hinoki Cypress''' (Designated Natural Monument of Azumino City)
== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * ''Shinshu Culture Series: Temples and Shrines''. 1981. [[Shinano Mainichi Shimbun]]. * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites, Vol. 9: Mino, Hida, Shinano|publisher=[[Hakusuisha]]|year=2000}}

== Ofune Matsuri == The annual [[Ofune Matsuri]] (Ship Festival) in April is a vibrant celebration featuring a massive, ship-shaped float<ref>[https://www.city.azumino.nagano.jp/site/mizumonogatari/2491.html Azumino City Ofune Matsuri Information]</ref>.

== location == * Nagano Prefecture, Azumino City, Misato-On 5932
